watchFor: AVOID: Feb 11: 0 wins on 3 trades (-$4,757) — likely averaging down or ignoring stop losses. This single day erased multiple good days.
AVOID: Feb 13: 2 trades after a bad Feb 12 — high risk of revenge trading. One loss of ~$2,381 on that day suggests oversizing.
AVOID: Cumulative loss count (18) nearly double win count (11) — selection criteria or entry timing needs refinement
tomorrowRules: Check market regime before opening the platform — only engage in RISK_ON or clearly trending conditions. Vix is at 23.72, elevated — size down 25-50% from normal.
Hard daily loss limit: if down more than $1,500 in day account, close platform for the day. Feb 11's $4,757 loss is what happens without this rule.
Require 2 confluent signals before entry — regime alignment + price action setup. No single-signal trades.
If first trade of the day loses, pause 30 minutes before considering a second trade. Do not immediately re-enter to 'get it back.'

dailyLessons.gradingReason: No trades today in a NEUTRAL market regime — this is actually disciplined behavior. Sitting out when conditions don't present a clear edge is a skill, not a failure. However, the underlying cumulative stats (37.9% win rate, 18 losses vs 11 wins) remain a concern that needs addressing.
keyLessons: Discipline to stay flat in NEUTRAL market conditions is correct — not every day needs a trade. Protect capital by only engaging when the regime and setup align.

Your cumulative win rate of 37.9% is below the threshold needed to be profitable unless your average win significantly exceeds your average loss. Review your recent losing trades (Feb 10-13: -$7,627 combined) to identify if you're cutting winners too early or letting losers run.

Feb 11 (-$4,757, 0/3) and Feb 10 (-$2,870, 0/1) followed by Feb 12 (-$845, 2/6) shows a pattern of consecutive loss days — identify what market condition or personal state triggered overtrading on Feb 12 after two bad days.

dailyLessons.riskAssessment: Today: excellent — zero exposure in an unfavorable regime. Cumulative risk management is the real concern. Losing days in Feb averaged -$2,600 per losing day while winning days averaged +$2,050 per winning day, meaning losses are outpacing wins on a per-day basis. Stop losses are either too wide or not being honored.
dailyLessons.emotionalCheck: No trades today eliminates emotional risk for this session. However, the Feb 10-13 sequence (four consecutive losing days totaling ~-$10,472) is a red flag for a previous emotional spiral. Watch for FOMO if the market makes a strong move tomorrow — the urge to 'make back' losses from two weeks ago may still be present subconsciously. The $20,632 day on Feb 6 may also be creating unrealistic daily expectations, leading to oversizing on subsequent days.
